title: The Scale-Sharing Test
subtitle: Can Nomad's favorite kind of business be measured as customer value plus durable economics?
research_question: Do companies that Nomad framed as sharing scale economies with customers show a measurable pattern of lower customer prices or better customer value, rising volume/revenue, and durable economics, without needing high near-term margins to make the investment work?
why_this_maps_to_memory: The local Nomad dataset marks Consumer Value Proposition in 24/24 letters.
Patience & Long-Term Horizon appears in 20/24 letters.
The strongest recurring examples include Costco, Amazon, Wal-Mart, Stagecoach, and Berkshire Hathaway.
The phrase 'scale-economics shared' appears in the Amazon/AirAsia/Costco context extracted from the letters.

seed_companies.name: Costco
seed_companies.nomad_thesis_seed: This is a very simple and honest consumer proposition in the sense that the membership fee buys the customer's loyalty (and is almost all profit) and Costco in exchange sells goods whils t just…
seed_companies.first_context: This is a very simple and honest consumer proposition in the sense that the membership fee buys the customer's loyalty (and is almost all profit) and Costco in exchange sells goods whils t just…

seed_companies.name: Amazon
seed_companies.nomad_thesis_seed: Air Asia is an example of scale -economics shared, which like Amazon, Costco, Carpetright and elements of other businesses in the portfolio (Geico, Nebraska Furniture Mart) have come to dominate…
seed_companies.first_context: In valuing the business at these prices, as occurred last summer, investors are saying to Amazon management “your growth spending has no value, you may as well turn yourself into a cash cow”!

seed_companies.name: Wal-Mart
seed_companies.nomad_thesis_seed: The moat thesis still needs a second-pass read anchored on the original passages.
seed_companies.first_context: Heuristic One: “the company has low margins” (net profit margin is 1.7%, compared to Wal Mart at 3.6% and Target at 4.2%).

seed_companies.name: Stagecoach
seed_companies.nomad_thesis_seed: This time I would like to discuss our investment in Stagecoach (8.6% of Partnership assets) and Costco Wholesale (3.1% of Partnership assets).
seed_companies.first_context: This time I would like to discuss our investment in Stagecoach (8.6% of Partnership assets) and Costco Wholesale (3.1% of Partnership assets).

seed_companies.name: Berkshire Hathaway
seed_companies.nomad_thesis_seed: The moat thesis still needs a second-pass read anchored on the original passages.
seed_companies.first_context: Souter began cutting away the weak businesses, a process Charlie Munger, vice Chairman of Berkshire Hathaway refers to as the “cancer surgery approach”.

hypotheses.claim: Scale-sharing companies should show customer-value pressure before accounting quality is obvious.
hypotheses.observable: Category prices, retail sales/volume, revenue growth, membership/subscription or unit indicators.

hypotheses.claim: The market underprices these companies when growth spending suppresses near-term margins.
hypotheses.observable: Drawdowns, valuation multiples, revenue growth, operating margin path, earnings-call language.

hypotheses.claim: Patience matters most when rates and macro conditions make distant payoff harder to hold.
hypotheses.observable: Returns from first mention across rate/inflation regimes and drawdown/recovery windows.

factiq_repo_basis.used_for: Confirmed FactIQ supports official statistics, market data, earnings-call intelligence, shareable charts, and reports.
Confirmed workflow: catalog, dataset search, SQL, market data, earnings search, then chart/report publishing.
Mapped likely schemas: BLS, Census, BEA, World Bank/IMF, and market/earnings tools.
Defined output as an evidence report with claim-titled charts and lineage.

factiq_questions.question: From first Nomad mention, did COST/AMZN/WMT outperform while fundamentals compounded?
Did customer-facing price/value indicators move differently for retail/ecommerce categories?
Does earnings-call language reveal the same tradeoff Nomad cared about: lower prices now, larger ecosystem later?
charts: examples table by company and period

starter_queries.label: Find CPI retail/category data in FactIQ
starter_queries.query: CPI retail prices apparel food household ecommerce consumer goods

starter_queries.label: Find Census retail sales data
starter_queries.query: retail sales ecommerce warehouse clubs department stores

starter_queries.label: Find company market and fundamentals data
starter_queries.query: SYMBOL_SEARCH / OVERVIEW / INCOME_STATEMENT / EARNINGS for COST, AMZN, WMT

starter_queries.label: Search earnings calls for scale-sharing language
starter_queries.query: price value customer savings scale efficiency membership flywheel

first_output.working_title: Were Nomad's scale-sharing companies underpriced because margins were the wrong signal?
sections: Nomad's seed thesis from the letters
Market and fundamentals after first mention
Earnings-call language: value, price, scale, and reinvestment
What this supports, what remains interpretive
